summary: Front‑of‑board matrix capture with brilliant mids, crisp cymbals and a solid set that hits Terrapin Station, Shakedown Street and Morning Dew.
review: The recording is a front‑of‑board (FOB) matrix taken on Nakamichi 700 tape, delivering an open sound with excellent mid‑range, highs and bass plus clear PA and cymbal definition. The show itself is praised as well‑played from start to finish, featuring standout moments like Terrapin Station, Shakedown Street, Not Fade Away and a surprise‑filled Morning Dew.

ai_rating.rationale: One detailed review gives high marks for both performance (strong song flow and many highlights) and technical quality (excellent FOB matrix sound), leading to a combined rating around 4½.

recording_quality.technical_notes: FOB capture on Nakamichi 700 tape; open, bright mids, clear highs and solid bass. PA is loud and clear (likely mono) and cymbal definition – especially ride bell – is outstanding.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set mixes new and classic material with energy; second set finds a groove, building through Shakedown St., Samson and Terrapin Station before a high‑octane finish featuring Throwing Stones, Not Fade Away and a crowd‑loving Morning Dew.

band_member_comments.Drums: Cymbal definition is clear; ride cymbal bell audible throughout the show.
